name: rasa.domain.soccer.goalkeeping
description: RasaOS Domain · Soccer Goalkeeping. Coaching foundation for soccer goalkeeping — technique, shot-stopping, distribution, positioning, training drills, match preparation, and keeper development across age/level.

rasa.note: Forked from domain-core (template baseline). Fill in shape_pattern + populate content/. For domain kind: review canon Spec §6 kind-specific shape variation for required structure.
rasa.naming: FIRST sub-namespaced domain (canon precedent, 2026-06-25). Canonical name rasa.domain.soccer.goalkeeping uses a dotted soccer.<specialty> hierarchy — valid because the schema ElementName first-party identifier is '.+'. Folder + repo use the dashed form (domain-soccer-goalkeeping); folder↔name is intentionally NOT reconstructable here, so tooling must read the dotted name from rasa.json#name (refresh-registry does). Alias rasa.domain.soccer-goalkeeping retained for flat-form discoverability. Future siblings under this hierarchy: rasa.domain.soccer.<striking|tactics|fitness|...>.

element.description: Files mirrored into the install target per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7. Shape scaffold (SHAPE.md, skills/, rules/, agents/) ships opt-in — registered for check-manifest coverage but not installed automatically; forks change policy as they populate content/. The v1.1.0 output-style-enforcement files ship file-replace (Element-owned, installed but inert until the consumer opts in) plus opt-in docs.

files.note: Fork-time guide explaining toolkit vs structural shape patterns. Not installed into consumer projects. Forks should delete this from their copy and replace with content/README.md describing what's actually shipped.
Toolkit-shape scaffold subdir. Forks that adopt the toolkit pattern populate this and change policy to directory-mirror. Structural-shape forks delete this entry + the directory.
Toolkit-shape scaffold subdir. Forks adopting toolkit add rule .md files here + change policy. Note canonical install target is .claude/ root (rules install flat per domain-code precedent).
Toolkit-shape scaffold subdir for Claude subagent definitions. Forks adopting toolkit add agent .md files here + change policy to directory-mirror.
How-to for the 3-layer response-format enforcement pattern (output style + CLAUDE.md pointer + opt-in Stop hook). Fork-time doc; not installed into consumer projects.

Stop hook — the 'hard' layer. Installed but INERT until the consumer wires it via settings (see settings.snippet.json) AND creates .claude/output-style.enforce.json. Element-owned generic code; refreshed on Element upgrade.
Example enforcement config + per-session toggle template. Copy to .claude/output-style.enforce.json to turn the Stop hook ON; delete that copy to turn it OFF.
settings.json Stop-hook registration snippet. Doc only; the consumer merges it once by hand. Not installed automatically.

seed.description: One-time files copied at install only. Per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7.

files.note: Per-project Claude session contract. Lives in install-target root; project-owned forever. Placeholder-free (Element identity lives in .claude/rasa.lock.json — current install-policy taxonomy doesn't substitute placeholders in skip-if-exists files; flagged in v0.1.0 AUDIT).

Stamped with the Element HEAD commit SHA at init time. Lockfile shape per canon Spec §6 (Connection Contract). Substitutes {{ELEMENT_NAME}}, {{ELEMENT_KIND}}, {{ELEMENT_VERSION}}, {{ELEMENT_REPO}}, {{ELEMENT_BRANCH}}, {{ELEMENT_SHA}}, {{TODAY}} placeholders via bin/init's init-only-with-sha handler.

Starter response-format output style — the 'soft' layer (v1.1.0). Copied once; project owns it. Select with /output-style rasa-default or outputStyle in settings. Placeholder-free per the documented skip-if-exists policy gap.

scaffold.description: Empty directories created at init. v1.0.0 ships none — forks add as needed.
